steps.title: Use the margin of error
steps.content: The survey estimate is $41\%$, and the margin of error is $6\%$. A margin of error gives a range of plausible values around the estimate, not one exact population percentage.

steps.title: Find the interval
steps.content:
Compute the lower and upper ends by subtracting and adding the margin of error.
$$41\%-6\%=35\%$$
$$41\%+6\%=47\%$$
So plausible values for the true percent are between $35\%$ and $47\%$. Statements outside this interval or statements using counts instead of percentages do not match the margin-of-error interpretation. The answer is C.
